evals.name: ops-metrics-framework-design
evals.prompt: Design an operational metrics framework for our mid-size fintech company. We have 15 vendors, process about 50,000 transactions daily, and are FCA regulated. The COO wants a dashboard with no more than 10 metrics that tells the operational story. We need coverage across vendor management, process operations, compliance, and incidents. Make sure there's a good mix of leading and lagging indicators.
evals.expected_output: A complete operational metrics framework with metric definitions covering vendor management, process operations, compliance, and incidents. Each metric has type (leading/lagging), owner, RAG thresholds, and measurement frequency. A recommended top 10 dashboard selection with rationale.
expectations: Output begins with the mandatory header containing TASK, DOCUMENT TYPE, CONFIGURATION, DATE, OWNER, REVIEW DATE fields
Document type is identified as Framework
Metrics are organized by domain: vendor management, process operations, compliance, and incidents
Each metric includes: what it measures, why it matters, type (leading/lagging), formula, data source, measurement frequency, owner, and RAG thresholds
At least one leading indicator is included for each major domain area
RAG thresholds define specific values for green, amber, and red with escalation actions for red
The recommended top 10 (or fewer) dashboard selection is provided
No more than 10 metrics are recommended for the executive dashboard
Metric owners are named roles (not 'the team')
Every metric has a defined formula (no ambiguous calculations)

evals.name: metric-definition-sla-compliance
evals.prompt: Define a metric for 'Vendor SLA Compliance Rate'. We have 15 vendors each with different SLA targets (uptime, response time, resolution time). I need the full metric definition including formula, thresholds, and what actions to take when it goes red. We measure monthly and report to the COO.
evals.expected_output: A complete metric definition card for Vendor SLA Compliance Rate with formula, data source, RAG thresholds specific to the context (15 vendors, monthly measurement), and escalation actions.
expectations: Output begins with the mandatory header containing TASK, DOCUMENT TYPE, CONFIGURATION, DATE, OWNER, REVIEW DATE fields
Document type is identified as Metric Definition
Metric name is clear and descriptive
What it measures and why it matters are explained in plain language
Type is classified (lagging indicator)
Formula is explicit and unambiguous (e.g. SLA targets met / total SLA targets across all vendors)
Data source is specified
Measurement frequency is monthly as requested
GREEN threshold is defined with a specific value or range
AMBER threshold is defined with investigation guidance
RED threshold is defined with specific escalation action and timeframe
Trend direction is specified (higher is better)

evals.name: monthly-ops-report
evals.prompt:
Generate a monthly operations report for January 2025. Here's the data:
- Vendor SLA compliance: 94% (was 97% in Dec, target 95%) -- 2 vendors breached
- Process error rate: 0.3% (was 0.2% in Dec, target <0.5%)
- Open audit findings: 4 (was 6 in Dec) -- closed 2, no new ones
- MTTR P1 incidents: 2.1 hours (was 1.8 hours in Dec, target <2 hours)
- Change failure rate: 8% (was 5% in Dec, target <10%)
- Obligation currency rate: 91% (was 89% in Dec, target 95%)
- Corrective action completion: 75% (was 80% in Dec, target 90%)
- Key person dependency: 3 roles (was 3 in Dec, target <2)
evals.expected_output: A structured monthly operations report with headline status, metrics table with RAG status and trend arrows, key issues section for red metrics, watch items for amber metrics, and upcoming actions.
expectations: Output begins with the mandatory header
A headline status is provided (e.g. WATCH ITEMS or ACTION REQUIRED based on the data)
A metrics table is present with columns: Metric, Status (G/A/R), This Month, Last Month, Trend, Action
Vendor SLA compliance (94%) is correctly assessed against 95% target -- should be amber or red since below target
MTTR (2.1 hours) is correctly flagged as red/amber since it exceeds the 2-hour target
Obligation currency rate (91%) is flagged since below 95% target
Corrective action completion (75%) is flagged since well below 90% target
KEY ISSUES section addresses the red metrics with specific actions
WATCH ITEMS section addresses amber metrics with monitoring rationale
UPCOMING section identifies actions needed in the next 30 days
Trends are correctly identified (vendor SLA declining, audit findings improving, etc.)
